title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an evidence-backed incident resolution packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Wireless Performance Faults trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.

content.text: For Net Wireless Performance Faults, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Wireless Performance Faults precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Increasing AP transmit power fixes weak coverage without affecting clients or roaming.

stimulus.title: Net Wireless Performance Faults case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Increasing AP transmit power fixes weak coverage without affecting clients or roaming.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity. Artifact under review: a complete first version of an evidence-backed incident resolution packet while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Wireless Performance Faults, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Performance Faults applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.
